body.content:
Kyoto Two-Day Itinerary - Inside Kyoto
Two days in Kyoto allows you to see a lot, but you still have to work efficiently. This itinerary is the way to make the most of two days in Kyoto.
![A couple walks through the bright red Fushimi Inari Taisha, Kyoto](https://photos.smugmug.com/photos/i-WWQHmLm/0/5fd5bb4e/L/i-WWQHmLm-L.jpg "A couple walks through the bright red Fushimi Inari Taisha, Kyoto")
Fushimi-Inari Taisha Shrine © [66321334@N00](https://www.flickr.com/photos/66321334@N00/9629639143)
### First: A Few Things to Keep in Mind
This two-day route takes you through Kyoto’s main sightseeing districts: [Southern Higashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/southern-higashiyama) and [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama). This is [a good route to do in the off-season (summer or winter)](https://www.insidekyoto.com/whens-the-best-time-to-go-to-kyoto). In high-season (spring and fall), it will be too crowded for comfort. If you will be in Kyoto in high-season, or simply like quieter spots, check out our [Off the Beaten Track Itineraries](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-off-the-beaten-track-itineraries). If you’ll be in town [during cherry blossom season](https://www.insidekyoto.com/when-do-cherry-blossoms-bloom-in-kyoto), check out our [Kyoto Cherry Blossom Itinerary](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-cherry-blossom-itinerary).
Top Activities In Kyoto
[![A woman in Japanese kimono and makeup poses with a parasol in a traditional Kyoto street, looking directly into the camera](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "A woman in Japanese kimono and makeup poses with a parasol in a traditional Kyoto street, looking directly into the camera")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kimono-rental-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-01)[Kimono Rental
In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kimono-rental-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-02)Rent a kimono and
hire a photographer
for the afternoon[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kimono-rental-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-03)
[![Head priest Miyazaki-san, wearing robes, talks with a visitor in the intricately-decorated main hall of the zen Kosei-ji Temple in Kyoto, Japan](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Head priest Miyazaki-san, wearing robes, talks with a visitor in the intricately-decorated main hall of the zen Kosei-ji Temple in Kyoto, Japan")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/zen-meditation-at-kosei-ji-temple-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-01)[Kosei-ji Temple
Zen Meditation](https://www.insidekyoto.com/zen-meditation-at-kosei-ji-temple-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-02)Experience zazen
(seated Zen meditation)
at a Kyoto temple[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/zen-meditation-at-kosei-ji-temple-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-03)
[![A kneeling woman in traditional Japanese outfit raises a cup during a Kyoto tea ceremony](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "A kneeling woman in traditional Japanese outfit raises a cup during a Kyoto tea ceremony")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-tea-ceremony?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-01)[Kyoto Tea
Ceremony](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-tea-ceremony?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-02)The home of the tea
ceremony remains the
best place to try it[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-tea-ceremony?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-03)
[![Red torils donated by Kansai Television flank the pathway through Fushimi-Inari Taisha Shrine in Kyoto, Japan](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Red torils donated by Kansai Television flank the pathway through Fushimi-Inari Taisha Shrine in Kyoto, Japan")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-01)[Fushimi-Inari
Hike](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-02)Hike to the summit
of Mount Inari-san and
visit the shrines[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Activities-Grid-03)
### Itinerary Summary:
* **Duration:** Two days of 6- to 8-hour touring.
* **Areas Covered:** [Southern Higashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/southern-higashiyama), [Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto), [Fushimi](https://www.insidekyoto.com/exploring-fushimi-kyotos-sake-district), [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama), [Northwest Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/northwest-kyoto).
* **Sights Visited:** [Kiyomizu-dera Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kiyomizu-dera-temple), [Sannen and Ninen-zaka Area](https://www.insidekyoto.com/ninen-zaka-and-sannen-zaka-preserved-districts), [Chion-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/chion-in-temple), [Shoren-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shoren-in-temple), [Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto), [Nishiki Market](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nishiki-market-downtown-kyoto), [Fushimi-Inari-Taisha Shrine](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-taisha-shrine), (optional: [Gion Shimbashi](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shimbashi-geisha-district-in-the-evening)); [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama), [Kinkaku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kinkaku-ji-temple-the-golden-pavilion) and [Daitoku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/daitoku-ji-temple).
* **Route Map:** Scroll down for a route map.
##### Hire A Travel Expert To Plan Your Japan Itinerary
If you would like to hire a travel expert to help you plan your own customised Japan itinerary, [please contact my company Japan Travel Consulting](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-travel-consulting) to discuss your needs.
##
Check Hotel Availability
###
Destination
#### Check-in date
#### Check-out date
![Booking.com](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='150'%20height='0'%20viewBox='0%200%20150%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
##### Day 1: [Southern Higashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/southern-higashiyama), [Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto) and [Fushimi-Inari](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-taisha-shrine)
![Tourists with parasols visit the stone steps and front gate of Kiyomizu-dera Temple, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Tourists with parasols visit the stone steps and front gate of Kiyomizu-dera Temple, Kyoto")
The front gate of Kiyomizu-dera Temple © [osakajock](https://www.flickr.com/photos/osakajock/230584573)
**8:30am:** [Kiyomizu-dera Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kiyomizu-dera-temple)
Take a bus or taxi to the Higashiyama-Gojo intersection. The closest bus stop is Gojozaka. Walk up Gojo-zaka, which is the main street that leads east, up the hill, to [Kiyomizu-dera Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kiyomizu-dera-temple). Most likely, you can just follow the people. Halfway up, you can bear right up Chawan-zaka, which gets you away from most of the cars and buses. Enter the temple and fully explore the temple. Don’t forget to visit the wonderful Tainai-Meguri.
![Ninen-zaka and Sannen-zaka steps leading up to the old-fashioned architecture and quiet streets of Kiyomizu-dera in Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Ninen-zaka and Sannen-zaka steps leading up to the old-fashioned architecture and quiet streets of Kiyomizu-dera in Kyoto")
Sannen-zaka Hill © [hslo](https://www.flickr.com/photos/hslo/4668611291)
**9:30am:** [Sannen-zaka and Ninen-zaka](https://www.insidekyoto.com/ninen-zaka-and-sannen-zaka-preserved-districts), then [Maruyama-koen Park](https://www.insidekyoto.com/maruyama-koen-park)
After exploring Kiyomizu-dera, exit via the front of the temple and walk down Matsubara-dori Street (lined with shops) to reach [the lovely preserved district of Sannen-zaka Hill](https://www.insidekyoto.com/ninen-zaka-and-sannen-zaka-preserved-districts). Continue down the street to where it flattens out and then go a bit further and take a right down into Ninen-zaka, another lovely preserved district. You will come to a larger cross street with cars. Go left down the hill then quickly turn right into [Nene-no-Michi, which is mostly free from cars](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nene-no-michi-lane). Follow this north, with one quick right-left jog to reach [Maruyama-koen Park](https://www.insidekyoto.com/maruyama-koen-park).
![Long view of path and Chion-in Temple Main Hall on a sunny day and a backdrop of trees](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Long view of path and Chion-in Temple Main Hall on a sunny day and a backdrop of trees")
Chion-in Temple Main Hall © [lucamascaro](https://www.flickr.com/)
**10:30am**: [Chion-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/chion-in-temple)
Exit the north side of [Maruyama-koen](https://www.insidekyoto.com/maruyama-koen-park) and follow the street north to the impressive main gate of [Chion-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/chion-in-temple). Climb the steep steps and enter the wide courtyard of Chion-in. Enter the main hall and sit down to soak up the amazing atmosphere (just remember that this is a religious building). Return down the steps and continue north.
![View of hallway in Keage Station, Kyoto Municipal Subway Tozai Line](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "View of hallway in Keage Station, Kyoto Municipal Subway Tozai Line")
Tozai subway line station © [: : Ys [waiz] : :](https://flic.kr/p/ZT4kDo) / [CC BY-ND 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nd/2.0/)
**11:00am:** [Shoren-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shoren-in-temple)
You’ll soon pass the enormous camphor trees in front of [Shoren-in Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shoren-in-temple). Enter the temple and enjoy a nice cup of green tea and a sweet while looking out over the garden.
Recommended Kyoto Accommodation
[![Seikoro](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/seikoro-ryokan.en-gb.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-01)[Seikoro](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/seikoro-ryokan.en-gb.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-02)Ryokan[More Info](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/seikoro-ryokan.en-gb.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-03)
[![Aoi Hotel Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/aoi-kyoto.en-gb.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-04)[Aoi Hotel Kyoto](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/aoi-kyoto.en-gb.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-05)Kyoto Townhouse[More Info](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/aoi-kyoto.en-gb.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-06)
[![Dormy Inn](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/dormy-inn-premium-kyoto-ekimae.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-07)[Dormy Inn](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/dormy-inn-premium-kyoto-ekimae.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-08)Mid Range[More Info](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/dormy-inn-premium-kyoto-ekimae.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-09)
[![Gojo Guest House](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/gojo-guest-house.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-10)[Gojo Guest House](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/gojo-guest-house.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-11)Low Price Rooms[More Info](https://www.booking.com/hotel/jp/gojo-guest-house.html?aid=965263;label=IKGridHotelResponsivePopular-12)
**11:45:** Travel to [Downtown Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto)
Exit Shoren-in and walk north (downhill) to reach Sanjo-dori Street. Walk a short distance west (left) on Sanjo-dori to reach Higashiyama Station on [the Tozai subway line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-subways). Take it two stops west and get off at Kyoto Shiyakushomae (downtown).
**Noon:** [Lunch Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto)
There’s an almost endless selection of places to eat downtown. For some picks, check out our [Downtown Kyoto District page](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto).
![Customers wander along a colourful aisle of Nishiki Market, Kyoto, looking at the food stalls](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Customers wander along a colourful aisle of Nishiki Market, Kyoto, looking at the food stalls")
Nishiki Market © [izzonme](https://www.flickr.com/photos/izzonme/18626692828)
**1:00pm:** [Nishiki Market](https://www.insidekyoto.com/nishiki-market-downtown-kyoto)
After eating lunch, make your way to the east end of Nishiki Market (where it joins the [Teramachi Shopping Arcade](https://www.insidekyoto.com/teramachi-shinkyogoku-shopping-arcades-kyoto)). It’s usually crowded here, even during the off-season. Hint: If you get tired of the crowds, you can bail by walking one block south and continuing along Shijo-dori Street.
![Customers pass cake counters in the Daimaru Department Store Food Floor, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='532'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20532'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Customers pass cake counters in the Daimaru Department Store Food Floor, Kyoto")
Daimaru Department Store Food Floor, Kyoto – image © Chris Rowthorn
**2:00pm:** [Daimaru Basement Food Floor](https://www.insidekyoto.com/daimaru-basement-food-floor-kyoto)
After you emerge from the west end of Nishiki, continue straight for a block and you’ll see Daimaru Department Store on your left. Go down the stairs in front of the entrance and enter the food floor. Explore the food floor and be sure to check out the Japanese sweets and tea section.
Practical Kyoto
[![Kyoto temple building set against a canopy of crimson and russet fall foliage](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "Kyoto temple building set against a canopy of crimson and russet fall foliage")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-itineraries?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-01)[Kyoto
Itineraries](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-itineraries?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-02)Make the most of your
trip with tailored
Kyoto itineraries[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-itineraries?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-03)
[![A Visit Japan Web app diagram showing the different QR codes to be shown at the different arrival gates at the airport](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "A Visit Japan Web app diagram showing the different QR codes to be shown at the different arrival gates at the airport")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/a-guide-to-mysos-visit-japan-web-and-japan-entry-procedures?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-01)[Guide To
Japan Entry](https://www.insidekyoto.com/a-guide-to-mysos-visit-japan-web-and-japan-entry-procedures?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-02)Full guide to the
recommended Visit
Japan Web app[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/a-guide-to-mysos-visit-japan-web-and-japan-entry-procedures?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-03)
[![A bed of leaves sitting on Ryoan-ji Temple Lake from the surrounding russet fall foliage](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "A bed of leaves sitting on Ryoan-ji Temple Lake from the surrounding russet fall foliage")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-01)[Packing List
For Japan](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-02)Comprehensive checklist
to make sure you take
what you need[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-03)
[![View of the Kamo-gawa River from a guest suite in the Ritz-Carlton hotel in central Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='auto'%20height='auto'%20viewBox='0%200%20auto%20auto'%3E%3C/svg%3E "View of the Kamo-gawa River from a guest suite in the Ritz-Carlton hotel in central Kyoto")](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-01)[Where To Stay
In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-02)Our guide to
Kyoto’s key areas for
places to stay[More Info](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=grid&utm_medium=link&utm_id=IK-Practical-Grid-03)
**2:30pm:** [Take the Keihan Line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-trains) to Fushimi-Inari Station
Exit Daimaru via the main entrance onto Shijo-dori Street. Walk east on Shijo-dori, [across the Kamo-gawa River](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kamo-gawa-riverside), and walk downstairs into Keihan Gion-Shijo Station. [Take the Keihan Line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-trains) south to Fushimi-Inari Station (all trains EXCEPT the limited express stop at Fushimi-Inari).
![A couple walks through the bright red Fushimi Inari Taisha, Kyoto](https://photos.smugmug.com/photos/i-WWQHmLm/0/5fd5bb4e/L/i-WWQHmLm-L.jpg "A couple walks through the bright red Fushimi Inari Taisha, Kyoto")
Fushimi-Inari Taisha Shrine © [66321334@N00](https://www.flickr.com/photos/66321334@N00/9629639143)
**3:00pm:** [Fushimi-Inari-Taisha Shrine](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-taisha-shrine)
The way is clearly marked from the station. Walk out of the station, take a left and walk up the hill, crossing the JR tracks. After crossing one relatively major street, you will see the first torii (gate) of the shrine. Walk up through this and you’ll shortly get to the main precinct of the shrine. Our [Fushimi-Inari Hike](https://www.insidekyoto.com/fushimi-inari-hike-kyoto) gives a detailed route description. We don’t suggest doing the whole route on this day, but going up as far as Shin-ike Pond is a good idea.
**Late Afternoon:** Return to hotel for Rest
By this point, you’ll definitely be in need of a rest. Take [either the Keihan Line or the JR Line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-trains) back from Fushimi-Inari to [central Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/central-kyoto), depending upon where in town you are staying.
**7:00pm:** Dinner Downtown
Downtown Kyoto has the best selection of restaurants in town. For some picks, check out our [Downtown Kyoto District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto) page.
![People wander along Gion Shirakawa street, Kyoto, atmospherically illuminated in the evening](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "People wander along Gion Shirakawa street, Kyoto, atmospherically illuminated in the evening")
Gion Shimbashi in the evening © [wakanmuri](https://www.flickr.com/photos/wakanmuri/5563797851)
**After Dinner:** Gion Shimbashi
After dinner, if you still have energy, a [walk through Gion](https://www.insidekyoto.com/walking-in-gion) is a great idea. Start from Shijo-Ohashi Bridge (the big bridge on Shijo-dori [over the Kamo-gawa River](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kamo-gawa-riverside)). At the east end of the bridge, cross Kawabata-dori and [walk north on the east side of Kawabata](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyotos-most-romantic-walks) for about 100 meters. You will soon see a tree-lined pedestrian lane. This is the end of Shirakawa-suji. Follow it east and you’ll get to [the Gion-Shimbashi District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/shimbashi-geisha-district-in-the-evening) (also known as Shirakawa), which is the most beautiful street in Kyoto. In busy seasons, it will be crowded with people. After checking it out, make your way through the alleys south to Shijo-dori and walk east to Shijo and cross over into Hanami-koji, another picturesque lane. This is [geisha territory](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-geisha), so keep your eyes peeled. We also have a detailed page [exploring Gion during the daytime](https://www.insidekyoto.com/walking-in-gion), which will help orientate you for an evening wander too.
##### Day 2: Arashiyama, Kinkaku-ji and Daitoku-ji
**8:30am:** Head west to Arashiyama by taxi, train or bus
Your choice of transport out to Arashiyama will depend on where you are staying and your willingness to take taxis. See our [Arashiyama District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama) page for transport information to Arashiyama (scroll to near the end of the page).
![A wide angle view of the garden and pond at Tenryu-ji Temple, Kyoto, will hills in the background](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='330'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20330'%3E%3C/svg%3E "A wide angle view of the garden and pond at Tenryu-ji Temple, Kyoto, will hills in the background")
The garden and pond at Tenryu-ji Temple © [neekohfi](https://www.flickr.com/photos/neekohfi/4517773734)
**9:00am:** [Tenryu-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/tenryu-ji-temple-arashiyama)
First, explore the superb Tenryu-ji Temple, being sure to check out both the inside of the main hall and the expansive stroll garden.
![The sun poking through the trees above a pathway through Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='535'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20535'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The sun poking through the trees above a pathway through Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Kyoto")
Arashiyama Bamboo Grove © [freakland](https://www.flickr.com/photos/freakland/4887832679)
**10:00am:** [Arashiyama Bamboo Grove](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama-bamboo-grove)
Leave Tenryu-ji by the north gate (accessible through the garden), and take a left, which will bring you into the famed Arashiyama Bamboo Grove. Take your time savoring the magical atmosphere here, while working slowly uphill.
![An atmospheric stone path through the moss and trees at Okochi Denjiro garden, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='530'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20530'%3E%3C/svg%3E "An atmospheric stone path through the moss and trees at Okochi Denjiro garden, Kyoto")
Garden at Okochi-Sanso Villa © [29237715@N05](https://www.flickr.com/photos/29237715@N05/7123925731)
**10:30am:** [Okochi-Sanso Villa](https://www.insidekyoto.com/okochi-sanso-villa-arashiyama)
At the top of the hill in the Bamboo Grove, you will see a small hut and a road leading up the hill into the forest. This is the entrance to the superb Okochi-Sanso Villa, the former home of a wealthy Japanese actor. Enter the villa and take your time exploring the garden. Be sure to hold onto the ticket they give you, because it will get you a nice cup of green tea and a sweet after the garden tour.
**11:30am:** Lunch in [Arashiyama](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama)
Leave Okochi-Sanso Villa and return to central Arashiyama. Eat a simple lunch of noodles or rice in one of the shokudo (noodle and rice restaurants) on the main strip. For some picks, see our [Arashiyama District](https://www.insidekyoto.com/arashiyama) page.
**12:30pm:** Taxi to Kinkaku-ji Temple
There really isn’t a good way by public transport, so from Arashiyama, [take a taxi](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-taxis) over to Kinkaku-ji Temple, the famed “Golden Pavilion” in Northwest Kyoto. A taxi from Arashiyama to Kinkaku-ji will cost around ¥3000. If you really want to go by public transport, here’s how: [Take the Keifuku-Randen Line train](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-trains) from Arashiyama Station (change to the Kitano Line at Kazuko-no-tsuji Station) to Kitano Hakubai-cho Station, and from there [you can take a bus](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-buses) (No. 204 or 205), or walk (about 20 minutes).
![The famous Golden Pavilion reflected in the way at Kyoto Kinkaku-Ji Temple](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='530'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20530'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The famous Golden Pavilion reflected in the way at Kyoto Kinkaku-Ji Temple")
Kinkaku-ji Temple © [43423301@N07](https://www.flickr.com/photos/43423301@N07/6217034711)
**1:00pm:** Kinkaku-ji Temple
Take your time strolling through [the grounds of Kinkaku-ji](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kinkaku-ji-temple-the-golden-pavilion). Yes, it will most likely be crowded at this time, but it almost always is.
**2:00pm:** Taxi to Daitoku-ji Temple
The next stop is the magical Zen world of Daitoku-ji Temple. The easiest and fastest way to get from Kinaku-ji to Daitoku-ji is a taxi, which will cost around ¥1000. If you prefer public transport, you can go from Kinkaku-ji to Daitoku-ji by bus (No 204/205). Or, you can walk there in about 20 minutes. Note that if you’re starting to feel templed out by this point, consider cutting out Daitoku-ji and just head back to [downtown Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto). Buses run for the Kinkakuji-michi stop to downtown and Kyoto Station.
![The zen temple of Zuiho-in, in the Daitoku-ji area, Kyoto](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='600'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20600'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The zen temple of Zuiho-in, in the Daitoku-ji area, Kyoto")
Zuiho-in Subtemple at Daitoku-ji Temple © [lejade](https://www.flickr.com/photos/lejade/2589670059)
**2:30pm:** [Daitoku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/exploring-daitoku-ji)
Explore the Zen world of Daitoku-ji Temple. Consider entering one of the subtemples like Koto-in. For full details on Daitoku-ji, see our [Exploring Daitoku-ji Temple](https://www.insidekyoto.com/exploring-daitoku-ji) page.
**3:00pm:** [Karasuma Subway Line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-subways) to Shijo Station
From Daitoku-ji, it’s about a 20-minute walk east on Kitaoji-dori Street to reach Kitaoji Station on the [Karasuma Subway Line](https://www.insidekyoto.com/kyoto-subways). This subway will get you to Shijo Station or Kyoto Station in a few minutes.
**Late Afternoon:** Rest at your hotel
By this point, you’ll certainly be in need of some down time. If you need suggestions for [where to stay in Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto), see our detailed rundown of [the best Kyoto districts and specific recommendations of places to stay](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto).
**6:30pm:** [Dinner Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto)
Downtown Kyoto has the best selection of restaurants in town. For some picks, check out our [Downtown Kyoto District page](https://www.insidekyoto.com/downtown-kyoto).
![The bright illuminations in Kyoto’s traditional Pontocho alley at night](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='800'%20height='534'%20viewBox='0%200%20800%20534'%3E%3C/svg%3E "The bright illuminations in Kyoto’s traditional Pontocho alley at night")
Pontocho by night © [aussieassault](https://www.flickr.com/photos/aussieassault/23525367196)
**8:00pm:** [Stroll Downtown](https://www.insidekyoto.com/pontocho-alley-kyoto)
After dinner, consider taking an evening stroll along [Pontocho Alley](https://www.insidekyoto.com/pontocho-alley-kyoto) or down the lovely stretch of Kiyamachi-dori south of Shijo-dori.
### Route Map
The Kyoto 2-Day Itinerary map shows the location of each of the places mentioned. You can view a [full screen version](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1HGEWFUHWVhlvkSY1VjKCb1XkvRy0rkE&usp=sharing) too.
### Kyoto Vacation Checklist
* [ESSENTIALS](https://www.insidekyoto.com/first-time-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-01) – For all the essentials in a brief overview, see my [First Time In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/first-time-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-02) guide
* [HOTELS](https://www.booking.com/city/jp/kyoto.html?aid=965263;label=IK-Checklist-03) – Check Kyoto accommodation availability on [Booking.com](https://www.booking.com/city/jp/kyoto.html?aid=965263;label=IK-Checklist-04) and [Agoda.com](https://www.agoda.com/partners/partnersearch.aspx?pcs=1&cid=1592349&city=1784&tag=IK-Checklist-05) - often you can book with no upfront payment and free cancellation
* [BULLET TRAIN](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826984&k_site=https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/81022-japan-rails/) – You can buy [shinkansen (bullet train) tickets online](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826984&k_site=https://www.klook.com/en-US/activity/81022-japan-rails/) from Klook - popular routes include [Tokyo to Kyoto](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1305/tokyo-to-kyoto-trains/), [Kyoto to Osaka](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1474/kyoto-to-osaka-trains/) and [Kyoto to Tokyo](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=826982&k_site=https://www.klook.com/rails-32/1012-japan/route-1307/kyoto-to-tokyo-trains/)
* [WHERE TO STAY](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-06) – Need tips on where to stay? See my one page guide [Where To Stay In Kyoto](https://www.insidekyoto.com/where-to-stay-in-kyoto?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-07)
* [PACKING LIST](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-08) – See my comprehensive [Packing List For Japan](https://www.insidekyoto.com/packing-list-for-japan?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-09)
* [SIM CARDS](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=2652&aff_adid=877200&k_site=https://www.klook.com/search/result/?query=japan%20esim) – You can buy an [eSim to activate in Japan](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=877279&k_site=https://www.klook.com/search/result/?query=japan%20esim) or buy a data-only SIM card online for collection when you arrive at [Tokyo's Narita or Haneda Airports](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=tokyo sim&searchSource=8&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-10>) or [Kansai International Airport](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=kansai sim&searchSource=8&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-11>). You can also rent an [unlimited data pocket wifi router](<https://www.getyourguide.com/s/?q=japan wifi&searchSource=7&partner_id=LAMDN2W&utm_medium=online_publisher&placement=content-middle&cmp=IK-Checklist-12>)
* [FLIGHTS](https://www.skyscanner.net/g/referrals/v1/flights/cheap-flights-to?adId=1100662&campaignId=13416&destination=tyoa&mediaPartnerId=1924024) – Compare Japan flight prices and timings to [find the best deals](https://www.skyscanner.net/g/referrals/v1/flights/cheap-flights-to?adId=1100662&campaignId=13416&destination=tyoa&mediaPartnerId=1924024)
* [JAPAN RAIL PASS](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=616549&k_site=https://www.klook.com/activity/1420-7-day-whole-japan-rail-pass-jr-pass/) – If you're making frequent train journeys during your visit, you might save money with [Japan Rail Pass](https://affiliate.klook.com/redirect?aid=1591&aff_adid=616549&k_site=https://www.klook.com/activity/1420-7-day-whole-japan-rail-pass-jr-pass/) – see [if it's worth it](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-rail-pass-is-it-worth-it?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-13) for you
* [TRAVEL INSURANCE](https://www.tkqlhce.com/click-7919142-15371527) – [World Nomads](https://www.tkqlhce.com/click-7647395-15371527) offers simple and flexible travel insurance. Buy at home or while traveling and claim online from anywhere in the world
* [WANT HELP?](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-travel-consulting?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-14) – Do you want help planning your trip? Chris Rowthorn and his team of Japan experts at [Japan Travel Consulting](https://www.insidekyoto.com/japan-travel-consulting?utm_source=checkbox&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=IK-Checklist-15) can help

### About InsideKyoto.com and Chris Rowthorn
* Chris Rowthorn is the co-founder of [InsideKyoto.com](https://www.insidekyoto.com) and has an unparalleled travel knowledge of Kyoto spanning three decades. Chris has written or commissioned and edited every page on the Inside Kyoto website.
* A former author of **Lonely Planet Kyoto** and **Lonely Planet Japan** guidebooks, Chris is also the director of the Kyoto walking tour company [Chris Rowthorn Tours](http://www.chrisrowthorn.com), and [Japan Travel Consulting](https://insideosaka.com/japan-travel-consulting/), where you can get bespoke travel advice for your Japan trip.
* [InsideKyoto.com](https://www.insidekyoto.com) has been cited by the **[New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/17/travel/booking-hotels-in-asia-the-getaway.html)** and **[The Times of London](https://www.thetimes.com/article/926c41d6-bb7d-11e5-8ae7-f2021079f4dd)**, among many other publications and websites.
